summary: Matrix recording with mixed‑quality sound captures a high‑energy show highlighted by a monster Shakedown Street and stellar second‑set jams.
review: The performance shines, especially the ripping Shakedown Street, soaring Dear Mr Fantasy/Hey Jude reprise, tight Brown Eyed Women, and vibrant Lovelight. While the matrix tape is muddy at the start and some listeners describe it as “like a Victrola,” the sound clears up after the first couple of minutes, making the music enjoyable despite occasional drop‑outs.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (70% weight) and note standout jams; recording quality is uneven but improves, giving a decent overall listening experience.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Early portion heavy on audience bleed and muffled; mid‑set clears up with audible instruments. Some reviewers report drop‑outs or “sounds like shit,” while others say it gets better after the first two minutes.

standout_songs: Goin' Down the Road Feelin' Bad
poor_songs: Hey Jude (sloppy version noted by a few reviewers)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is upbeat and sing‑along friendly, while the second set erupts into high‑octane jams; the show’s energy mirrors the chaotic “riot” atmosphere but the music stays tight and purposeful.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as energetic on guitar and vocals, flashing a grin during Women Are Smarter, delivering strong slide work and vocal grit on many songs.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is louder and driving, especially noted on Shakedown Street; described as “rambling and rumbling” but solidly supportive of the groove.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Weir) adds slide solos on Women Are Smarter and contributes vocal harmonies throughout; his presence is highlighted in several jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ is warm, punchy, and full of “electric fills,” delivering memorable solos on Lovelight and other numbers.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill & Mickey are aggressive yet cohesive, providing a steady bed for the band; drums/Space sections are praised as above‑average.
